notes: Baseline now concise (51 lines vs 147 in v1) — fast-path-routing no longer discriminates
v2 WITH skill: 21/21 (100%) vs v1: 20/20 (100%) — maintained perfect score with harder assertions
v2 WITHOUT skill: 14/21 (66.7%) vs v1: 12/20 (60%) — baseline improved on new eval-2 (GRADE, pedagogy)
Delta decreased v1→v2 (+40pp → +31pp) but assertions are now more honest: test utility, not just structure
NEW: with_skill is now FASTER than baseline (-33.8s avg) and uses only +13% more tokens. v1 was +33% time.
Eval-1 WITHOUT took 511s vs 260s WITH — baseline rambled into 300 lines of pathophysiology, skill focused output
Eval-3 no longer discriminates (6/6 both configs) — fast-path assertion needs revision or eval is validated
Discriminating assertions v2: narrative-synthesis, actionable-recommendation, comparative-table-with-implications, convergence-interpreted, depth-rubric-with-implications, cross-leg-synthesis
Baseline v2 now does real GRADE on clinical data (eval-2) — the eval-2 swap from definitional→clinical slide worked
Key qualitative win: WITH skill opens with narrative prose; WITHOUT opens with tables. Structure matches professor workflow.
